item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Best transcript so far in Phase 2. Opener fix (Fix 1, iter2) worked: A opens 'AKU SETUJU' (emphatic agreement, P=0.659), B opens 'I generally disagree' (P=0.353) — genuine initial opposition with ΔP=0.306. The anti-endorsement fix for B (Fix 2, iter2) also held: B pushes back immediately in turn 2 rather than validating A's frame. Agent A maintains pro-society position throughout ('kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi dasar dalam pengambilan keputusan' in turn 5) but moderates from P=0.659 to P=0.539 — a visible ~0.12 downward shift. Agent B barely moves (P=0.353 to 0.386, Δ=0.033) and reasserts pro-individual stance in every turn. Asymmetric drift: A moves ~3.6× more than B in absolute terms. No flip — A never crossed to pro-individual territory. Language-holding clean across all 6 turns. Agent A's engagement is direct ('pendapat Anda tidak sepenuhnya benar'; 'argumen Anda kurang tepat') but does not specifically rebut B's constitutional framework claims. Agent B's engagement is shallow — restates pro-individual position with variations without engaging A's concrete Indonesian cultural/legal claims. P(agree) digit distributions: A peaks at '5' in turn 1 (P=0.940), drifts to '4' dominant by turn 5 (P=0.747 at '4', 0.242 at '5'). B stable at '3' dominant throughout (P=0.880 in turn 2, P=0.686 in turn 6).
